subject: two questions about the twilight council

posts.body:
1. The board says that you 'craft with assemblies'. So, the idea of having them craft with enemies crafting pieces has been abandoned? Why? Or am I mis-reading this?

2. It appears that any opponent can flip an assembly to closed for no cost except loyalists for the Council? What the heck? Is there a once-per turn limit? Card cost? Requirement that you have to be at the assembly. I don't understand.

Clarification would be great. Thanks, all!

1. The board says that you 'craft with assemblies'. So, the idea of having them craft with enemies crafting pieces has been abandoned? Why? Or am I mis-reading this?

Yes that was abandoned as it was impossible to balance

2. It appears that any opponent can flip an assembly to closed for no cost except loyalists for the Council? What the heck? Is there a once-per turn limit? Card cost? Requirement that you have to be at the assembly. I don't understand.

There is no frequency limit or cost for the player activating Entreat. The hidden cost is that using it wantonly will supercharge the Bats' recruiting

MiceandMysticslover wrote:

1. The board says that you 'craft with assemblies'. So, the idea of having them craft with enemies crafting pieces has been abandoned? Why? Or am I mis-reading this?

[Edit: The following about Lilypad Diaspora when it was Twilight Council being asked about]

From https://rules.ledergames.com/?product=root
16.4.1 Craft. You may activate enclaves to craft cards (16.2.1.II).

MiceandMysticslover wrote:

2. It appears that any opponent can flip an assembly to closed for no cost except loyalists for the Council? What the heck? Is there a once-per turn limit? Card cost? Requirement that you have to be at the assembly. I don't understand.

That would be Negotiations.

16.2.5 Negotiations. Once during their turn, an enemy with faction pieces in a clearing with a Militant enclave may flip it to Peaceful. If they don't rule the enclave but another player does, the flipping player must give the ruler a card in order to flip the enclave.

From rules.ledergames.com

MiceandMysticslover wrote:

2. It appears that any opponent can flip an assembly to closed for no cost except loyalists for the Council? What the heck? Is there a once-per turn limit? Card cost? Requirement that you have to be at the assembly. I don't understand.

That would be Negotiations.

16.2.5 Negotiations. Once during their turn, an enemy with faction pieces in a clearing with a Militant enclave may flip it to Peaceful. If they don't rule the enclave but another player does, the flipping player must give the ruler a card in order to flip the enclave.

OP was asking about the Bats, and you are referencing the Frogs

OmegasSquared wrote:

OP was asking about the Bats, and you are referencing the Frogs

Oh thanks, that'll teach me to reply while not having woken up yet.

The answer is, there's no mistakes that's precisely how they work.

Play a couple of games and you should find they're well balanced (even a little strong imo)

OmegasSquared wrote:

1. The board says that you 'craft with assemblies'. So, the idea of having them craft with enemies crafting pieces has been abandoned? Why? Or am I mis-reading this?

Yes that was abandoned as it was impossible to balance

This disappoints me. How was it impossible to balance? It seems like it would encourage strategic placement of assemblies. Of course, they would occasionally struggle to craft but that seems like a reasonably thematic cost of playing without helpful allies. What is the problem? Where did they release this?

The answer to question 2 is very interesting. I wonder how the meta will turn out for that.

They already score from enemy cardboard, so strategic assemblies are still required.
The crafting thing was an issue because different matchups have vastly different crafting pieces. In a WA game it'd be fine, but against Cats, Vagabond and Keepers in Iron there may only ever be 3 crafting pieces on the board in the whole game.
Whereas the amount of cardboard is consistent across most matchups, in my afformentioned example, cats and badgers put down plenty of cardboard, it's just only a small percentage are crafting pieces.

Basically crafting pieces are ridiculously variable whereas cardboard (while still being variable) is far less so

Laterbot wrote:

Whereas the amount of cardboard is consistent across most matchups, in my afformentioned example, cats and badgers put down plenty of cardboard, it's just only a small percentage are crafting pieces.

Basically crafting pieces are ridiculously variable whereas cardboard (while still being variable) is far less so

Thanks for the clarification. I still would have liked the mechanic to show up somewhere. Maybe they choose which to use each turn? Or they use both?
Would be nice if they considered such nice crafting changes for the lizards 🤐😅

shadowhawk720 wrote:

Would be nice if they considered such nice crafting changes for the lizards 🤐😅

The lizards actually do have a small crafting buff in Homeland, specifically in relation to Lost City, the new Landmarks and Lilypad Diaspora clearings

How so? The Lilypad Diaspora seems like a nerf, not a buff.
